kernel: The evidence suggests intelligence is not one scalar quantity but a profile: <Domain_Breadth> <Conceptual_Composition> <Local_Reasoning> <Planning_Horizon> <Error_Recovery> <Tool_Use> <Learning_Rate> <Grounding> <Judgment> <Reliability>.
full: The evidence suggests intelligence is not one scalar quantity but a profile: <Domain_Breadth> <Conceptual_Composition> <Local_Reasoning> <Planning_Horizon> <Error_Recovery> <Tool_Use> <Learning_Rate> <Grounding> <Judgment> <Reliability>. A system can be extraordinary on several dimensions and structurally weak on others.

kernel: The paper moves evaluation from: <Fixed_Benchmark> → <Aggregate_Score> toward: <Novel_Task> → <Interactive_Probe> → <Perturbation> → <Revision> → <Failure_Analysis>.
full: The paper moves evaluation from: <Fixed_Benchmark> → <Aggregate_Score> toward: <Novel_Task> → <Interactive_Probe> → <Perturbation> → <Revision> → <Failure_Analysis>. This is closer to an oral examination than a standardized test, but it requires stronger procedural discipline than the paper supplies.

kernel: If next-token prediction provides broad local competence but weak global planning, the likely path forward is not merely a larger predictor.
full: If next-token prediction provides broad local competence but weak global planning, the likely path forward is not merely a larger predictor. It is a system that separates: <Generation> <Planning> <Memory> <Verification> <Tool_Execution> <World_Feedback> <Learning_From_Consequences>.

kernel: The paper’s “vision” experiments show that executable text can become a visual medium.
full: The paper’s “vision” experiments show that executable text can become a visual medium. GPT-4 does not cross from text into image through direct perception; it composes symbolic operations that cause images to appear. Intelligence here lies partly in controlling transformations between representational systems.

kernel: Calling a proprietary and inaccessible system an early AGI does institutional work.
full: Calling a proprietary and inaccessible system an early AGI does institutional work. It elevates one company’s model from product to historical threshold and grants its developers unusual authority to define intelligence, risk, evaluation, and the direction of future research.

kernel: Behavior does not carry its meaning on its surface.
full: Behavior does not carry its meaning on its surface. To understand an act is to reconstruct the layered social conditions under which that movement, phrase, object, or omission counts as the act it is.

kernel: <Observable_Event> → <Possible_Social_Codes> → <Actor_Intentions> → <Audience_Interpretations> → <Historical_Conditions> → <Institutional_Power> → <Competing_Frames> → <Provisional_Reading>.
full: <Observable_Event> → <Possible_Social_Codes> → <Actor_Intentions> → <Audience_Interpretations> → <Historical_Conditions> → <Institutional_Power> → <Competing_Frames> → <Provisional_Reading>.

kernel: Description and explanation cannot be cleanly separated.
full: Description and explanation cannot be cleanly separated. Choosing what to record, naming the action, arranging its context, and deciding which interpretation matters are already analytic acts.

kernel: A model that recognizes eyelid motion has achieved thin description.
full: A model that recognizes eyelid motion has achieved thin description. A model that identifies a wink requires social convention. A model that distinguishes satire, rehearsal, deception, and misrecognition requires recursive models of actors, audiences, histories, and power. The gap between perception and cultural understanding is therefore not simply a matter of adding more sensory data.

kernel: A world model that predicts physical state transitions can still fail to model social reality.
full: A world model that predicts physical state transitions can still fail to model social reality. The same bodily action can alter alliances, obligations, threats, jokes, or legal standing depending on the interpretive frame in which it occurs.

kernel: Combining text, image, sound, and action does not by itself produce thick understanding.
full: Combining text, image, sound, and action does not by itself produce thick understanding. Multimodality may enrich the observable surface while leaving the socially established code unresolved.

kernel: Correct classification should not be evaluated only against a label such as <wink>.
full: Correct classification should not be evaluated only against a label such as <wink>. It should be tested against competing plausible interpretations and require an account of which contextual evidence changes the classification.

kernel: Sora suggests that sufficiently scaled video prediction can acquire partial regularities of space, time, identity, and interaction without being explicitly programmed with objects or physical laws.
full: Sora suggests that sufficiently scaled video prediction can acquire partial regularities of space, time, identity, and interaction without being explicitly programmed with objects or physical laws. But this establishes a promising representational substrate-not yet a dependable world simulator.

kernel: <Renderer> produces convincing appearances.
full: <Renderer> produces convincing appearances. <Temporal_Renderer> preserves appearance across time. <Generative_World> supports editable and extendable scenes. <Simulator> predicts consequences of interventions. <Planner_World_Model> supports comparison among possible actions. Sora demonstrates substantial movement through the first three categories; the report extrapolates toward the fourth.

kernel: <Raw_Visual_Data> → <Compression_Network> → <Spacetime_Latents> → <Patch_Sequence> → <Diffusion_Transformer> → <Generated_Latents> → <Decoder> → <Visible_Video>.
full: <Raw_Visual_Data> → <Compression_Network> → <Spacetime_Latents> → <Patch_Sequence> → <Diffusion_Transformer> → <Generated_Latents> → <Decoder> → <Visible_Video>.

kernel: <User_Prompt> → <GPT_Expansion> → <Detailed_Caption> → <Text_Conditioning> → <Latent_Denoising> → <Video>.
full: <User_Prompt> → <GPT_Expansion> → <Detailed_Caption> → <Text_Conditioning> → <Latent_Denoising> → <Video>. The generated world is conditioned by a machine-authored interpretation of the request.

kernel: A useful model of the world must preserve different things for different purposes.
full: A useful model of the world must preserve different things for different purposes. Cinema requires visual continuity. Robotics requires intervention fidelity. Science requires measurable state. Social interaction requires meaning, intention, and norm recognition. One representation may not satisfy all four.

kernel: Sora can generate increasingly thick sensory surfaces while remaining socially thin.
full: Sora can generate increasingly thick sensory surfaces while remaining socially thin. It may render the movement of a wink without knowing whether the act is conspiracy, parody, rehearsal, flirtation, or neurological twitch.

kernel: The immediate product is not necessarily a physical simulator.
full: The immediate product is not necessarily a physical simulator. It is a reusable synthetic production environment for advertising, entertainment, design, prototyping, training data, and visualization. “World simulation” supplies the longer strategic horizon.

kernel: NVIDIA’s “world model” is best understood as a synthetic-experience operating system for physical AI rather than a singular internal representation of reality.
full: NVIDIA’s “world model” is best understood as a synthetic-experience operating system for physical AI rather than a singular internal representation of reality.

kernel: <Real_World_Data> → <Curation> → <Captioning_And_Search> → <Tokenization> → <World_Foundation_Model> → <Synthetic_Scenarios> → <Embodiment_Specific_Post_Training> → <Policy_Learning> → <Simulation_Validation> → <Physical…
full: <Real_World_Data> → <Curation> → <Captioning_And_Search> → <Tokenization> → <World_Foundation_Model> → <Synthetic_Scenarios> → <Embodiment_Specific_Post_Training> → <Policy_Learning> → <Simulation_Validation> → <Physical_Deployment> → <Failure_Data> → <Model_Update>.

kernel: <Renderer> creates plausible appearances.
full: <Renderer> creates plausible appearances. <Predictor> estimates future observations. <Simulator> applies intervention-conditioned state transitions. <Planner_Model> evaluates possible actions. <Policy> selects actions. <Controller> executes them through a specific body. <Verifier> determines whether predicted and actual consequences agree. The page frequently treats these as one continuum, but their failure modes differ.

kernel: The system aims to model physical state richly while risking social thinness.
full: The system aims to model physical state richly while risking social thinness. It may identify a person falling, raising a hand, entering a restricted zone, or moving unusually without knowing whether the act is accident, ritual, protest, parody, disability, or ordinary local practice.

kernel: OpenAI’s Sora report moves from video generation toward simulation through emergent continuity.
full: OpenAI’s Sora report moves from video generation toward simulation through emergent continuity. NVIDIA moves from simulation infrastructure toward video generation as a means of scaling physical training. One begins with rendering and claims a path to worlds; the other begins with physical AI and absorbs generation into the training stack.

kernel: The provider of the world foundation model controls the representational substrate through which downstream robots learn what counts as an object, hazard, normal trajectory, plausible human behavior, and successful action.
full: The provider of the world foundation model controls the representational substrate through which downstream robots learn what counts as an object, hazard, normal trajectory, plausible human behavior, and successful action.

kernel: Genie 3 turns generated media into a responsive environment.
full: Genie 3 turns generated media into a responsive environment. Its importance lies not primarily in better-looking video, but in making synthetic visual worlds answer actions quickly enough to become places of rehearsal.

kernel: <World_Description> + <Prior_Frames> + <Action_History> + <Current_User_Input> → <Next_Visual_State> → <New_Action> → <Extended_Trajectory>.
full: <World_Description> + <Prior_Frames> + <Action_History> + <Current_User_Input> → <Next_Visual_State> → <New_Action> → <Extended_Trajectory>.

kernel: <Navigation_Control> selects movement inside the current world.
full: <Navigation_Control> selects movement inside the current world. <World_Event_Control> alters the world’s conditions through language. This resembles a game in which the player and the dungeon master operate through different interfaces.

kernel: <Sora> demonstrates long-form visual generation and partial physical continuity.
full: <Sora> demonstrates long-form visual generation and partial physical continuity. <Genie_3> adds real-time action conditioning and short-term navigable persistence. The transition is: <Video_Of_A_World> → <World_That_Responds>.

kernel: The earlier World Models architecture learned a compact simulator and trained a small controller inside it.
full: The earlier World Models architecture learned a compact simulator and trained a small controller inside it. Genie 3 instead generates the perceptual environment itself in real time and allows a separate agent to act through that stream. The simulator is no longer merely latent; it becomes the agent’s visible sensorium.

kernel: Genie 3 can generate rich physical surroundings but remains culturally thin.
full: Genie 3 can generate rich physical surroundings but remains culturally thin. It may render a Victorian street, palace, festival, or ancient city without modeling the public codes through which actions there acquire social meaning.

kernel: Whoever designs the world generator also designs the agent’s formative experience.
full: Whoever designs the world generator also designs the agent’s formative experience. Dataset composition, prompt language, visual conventions, omitted hazards, and preferred scenarios become part of the future agent’s practical common sense.

kernel: A robotic world model should be understood as a counterfactual action instrument: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Action_Selection> → <Physical_Execution> → <Observed_Discrepancy> → <Model_Revision>.
full: A robotic world model should be understood as a counterfactual action instrument: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Action_Selection> → <Physical_Execution> → <Observed_Discrepancy> → <Model_Revision>.

kernel: <Renderer> creates plausible future appearances.
full: <Renderer> creates plausible future appearances. <Forecaster> predicts likely future observations. <Controllable_Model> distinguishes futures under different actions. <Simulator> sustains closed-loop interaction. <Evaluator> ranks predicted consequences. <Policy> selects actions. <Controller> realizes those actions through a particular body. The survey shows these roles increasingly merging, but they should remain separately testable.

kernel: The central metric should be <action-counterfactual fidelity>: Given the same initial state, does changing only the action produce the correctly different consequence?
full: The central metric should be <action-counterfactual fidelity>: Given the same initial state, does changing only the action produce the correctly different consequence? Visual realism is secondary when the system will control machinery.

kernel: The field is moving through: <External_World_Model> → <World_Model_As_Policy_Input> → <Shared_Predictive_Control_Backbone> → <Latent_Internalized_World_Model> → <Policy_And_Simulator_Co_Evolution>.
full: The field is moving through: <External_World_Model> → <World_Model_As_Policy_Input> → <Shared_Predictive_Control_Backbone> → <Latent_Internalized_World_Model> → <Policy_And_Simulator_Co_Evolution>. Each step improves integration while making independent verification more difficult.

kernel: Future robotics datasets need more than successful demonstrations.
full: Future robotics datasets need more than successful demonstrations. They require: <Failures> <Near_Misses> <Recovery_Trajectories> <Alternative_Actions> <Contact_And_Force> <Delayed_Consequences> <Human_Interruption> <Environment_Change>.

kernel: These systems model physical transitions more readily than social meaning.
full: These systems model physical transitions more readily than social meaning. A robot may predict that a raised hand precedes a movement while failing to distinguish greeting, warning, command, surrender, ritual, disability, or joke. Physical foresight can remain culturally thin.

kernel: Once a world model trains, evaluates, and filters robot behavior, it becomes a constitutional layer.
full: Once a world model trains, evaluates, and filters robot behavior, it becomes a constitutional layer. It determines which consequences are imaginable, which risks are visible, and which actions appear rational before the machine enters the world.

kernel: WorldDreamer proposes that a world model need not begin by predicting the next frame.
full: WorldDreamer proposes that a world model need not begin by predicting the next frame. It can instead learn a distribution over complete visual events and generate particular futures by selectively revealing, masking, and conditioning parts of that event.

kernel: <Images_And_Videos> → <VQGAN_Tokenization> → <Masked_Visual_Sequence> + <Text_Embedding> + <Action_Embedding> → <Spatial_Temporal_Patchwise_Transformer> → <Predicted_Tokens> → <Visual_Decoder> → <Generated_Or_Edited_Video>.
full: <Images_And_Videos> → <VQGAN_Tokenization> → <Masked_Visual_Sequence> + <Text_Embedding> + <Action_Embedding> → <Spatial_Temporal_Patchwise_Transformer> → <Predicted_Tokens> → <Visual_Decoder> → <Generated_Or_Edited_Video>.

kernel: <Text_To_Video> all visual tokens are masked.
full: <Text_To_Video> all visual tokens are masked. <Image_To_Video> the first frame is visible and later frames are masked. <Video_Inpainting> a spatial region is masked through time. <Video_Stylization> tokens are partially masked and reconstructed under a style prompt. <Action_To_Video> future visual tokens are predicted under control signals.

kernel: <Visual_Completion_Model> reconstructs masked appearances.
full: <Visual_Completion_Model> reconstructs masked appearances. <Video_Generator> produces plausible moving imagery. <Action_Conditioned_Predictor> distinguishes futures under controls. <World_Simulator> supports repeated closed-loop intervention. <Planner_Model> evaluates possible actions by their predicted consequences. WorldDreamer strongly demonstrates the first two and begins to demonstrate the third.

kernel: The prompt does not merely describe a video.
full: The prompt does not merely describe a video. It participates causally in filling missing visual tokens. Language becomes an operator over a partially specified visual world: <Description> → <Latent_Constraint> → <Token_Selection> → <Moving_Image>.

kernel: WorldDreamer and Sora both create common representations for heterogeneous visual media, but their generative logics differ: <WorldDreamer> reconstructs discrete masked tokens in parallel.
full: WorldDreamer and Sora both create common representations for heterogeneous visual media, but their generative logics differ: <WorldDreamer> reconstructs discrete masked tokens in parallel. <Sora> denoises continuous spacetime latent patches. Both turn visual media into transformer-processable units and infer world understanding from scalable video generation.

kernel: WorldDreamer can generate action-conditioned driving videos, but Genie attempts continuous real-time interaction.
full: WorldDreamer can generate action-conditioned driving videos, but Genie attempts continuous real-time interaction. The difference is between: <Action_Specified_Clip> and <Action_Response_Loop>.

kernel: Masked world modeling may be especially useful where multiple forms of conditioning, editing, reconstruction, and counterfactual completion must share one model.
full: Masked world modeling may be especially useful where multiple forms of conditioning, editing, reconstruction, and counterfactual completion must share one model. It is less obviously sufficient for irreversible closed-loop control where only past evidence is available.

kernel: The main research question is not whether robots need language or world models.
full: The main research question is not whether robots need language or world models. It is which representational path most efficiently converts human intent into physically valid change.

kernel: <VLA_Path> <Language_And_Image_Understanding> → <Action_Grounding> → <Control>.
full: <VLA_Path> <Language_And_Image_Understanding> → <Action_Grounding> → <Control>. <WAM_Path> <Language_Conditioned_Dynamics> → <Future_State_Or_Latent_Plan> → <Action_Recovery> → <Control>.

kernel: <Understanding_Expert> interprets language, objects, relations, and social context.
full: <Understanding_Expert> interprets language, objects, relations, and social context. <Predictive_Expert> models possible future states and action consequences. <Action_Expert> translates selected futures into embodiment-specific control. <Verifier> checks feasibility, uncertainty, safety, and real-world discrepancy. <Memory> preserves task state and consequences across long horizons.

kernel: The important boundary is where imagination stops: <Full_Video_At_Inference> <Partial_Latent_Rollout> <Single_Visual_Subgoal> <Compact_Future_Embedding> <Training_Only_Predictive_Objective>.
full: The important boundary is where imagination stops: <Full_Video_At_Inference> <Partial_Latent_Rollout> <Single_Visual_Subgoal> <Compact_Future_Embedding> <Training_Only_Predictive_Objective>. These may provide similar benefits at radically different latency and auditability costs.

kernel: WAMs exchange robot-data scarcity for video-compute abundance.
full: WAMs exchange robot-data scarcity for video-compute abundance. They may require fewer physical demonstrations while inheriting enormous costs in video collection, captioning, preprocessing, pretraining, storage, and high-latency inference.

kernel: A clean comparison must hold constant: <Robot_Data> <Embodiment> <Parameter_Count> <Pretraining_Compute> <Action_Head> <Inference_Budget> <Test_Distribution>.
full: A clean comparison must hold constant: <Robot_Data> <Embodiment> <Parameter_Count> <Pretraining_Compute> <Action_Head> <Inference_Budget> <Test_Distribution>. Without this, “VLA versus WAM” remains partly a contest between entire industrial stacks.

kernel: Video pretraining may teach what an action visually resembles without teaching what the action socially means.
full: Video pretraining may teach what an action visually resembles without teaching what the action socially means. A robot may model the movement required to hand over an object while missing whether the exchange is invitation, obligation, refusal, theft, ceremony, or joke.

kernel: Knowlton’s system establishes animation as a programmable relation among: <Visual_State> <Spatial_Operation> <Temporal_Exposure> <Camera_Configuration> <Storage> <Transformation> <Output_Medium>.
full: Knowlton’s system establishes animation as a programmable relation among: <Visual_State> <Spatial_Operation> <Temporal_Exposure> <Camera_Configuration> <Storage> <Transformation> <Output_Medium>.

kernel: <Program> → <Surface_State> → <Scanner_Operations> → <Movie_Macros> → <Camera_Call> → <Frame_Duration_Record> → <Microfilm_Master> → <Optical_Printing> → <Final_Film>.
full: <Program> → <Surface_State> → <Scanner_Operations> → <Movie_Macros> → <Camera_Call> → <Frame_Duration_Record> → <Microfilm_Master> → <Optical_Printing> → <Final_Film>.

kernel: <Scanner_Language> provides local reading, writing, movement, tests, and control flow.
full: <Scanner_Language> provides local reading, writing, movement, tests, and control flow. <Movie_Language> provides image-level drafting, compositing, transformation, storage, and temporal operations. <User_Macros> provide project-specific cinematic concepts built from the lower layers.

kernel: Animation occurs when a transformation exposes its intermediate states to the camera.
full: Animation occurs when a transformation exposes its intermediate states to the camera. <Hidden_Intermediate_States> produce an instantaneous edit. <Recorded_Intermediate_States> produce visible movement. The difference between editing and animation is therefore not the operation itself, but whether its process is inscribed as time.

kernel: The same machine infrastructure produces text, diagrams, and moving images.
full: The same machine infrastructure produces text, diagrams, and moving images. Characters are simultaneously: <Instructions_In_Code> <Symbols_In_Memory> <Glyphs_On_Film> <Grey_Value_Carriers>. Text and image are not separate media here; text technology is repurposed as a visual substrate.

kernel: The system contains a minimal executable world: <Surface> defines space.
full: The system contains a minimal executable world: <Surface> defines space. <Digit> defines local state. <Scanner> defines situated agency. <Test> defines perception. <Operation> defines action. <Camera> defines observation. <Frame_Count> defines time. It is not a predictive world model, but it is an explicit operational world in which visual agents alter state according to rules.

kernel: The enduring design principle is that a film language should expose a small number of composable visual verbs while allowing users to define higher-order cinematic operations.
full: The enduring design principle is that a film language should expose a small number of composable visual verbs while allowing users to define higher-order cinematic operations. The system’s power lies less in any single command than in the ability to compile new verbs from primitive ones.

kernel: Cybernetic cinema is not defined by synthetic imagery.
full: Cybernetic cinema is not defined by synthetic imagery. It is cinema in which perception and production form a recursive system: <Specification> → <Generation> → <Display> → <Perception> → <Evaluation> → <Modification> → <Regeneration>.

kernel: <Photographic_Cinema> records events staged before a camera.
full: <Photographic_Cinema> records events staged before a camera. <Computer_Animation> constructs frames through programmed operations. <Cybernetic_Cinema> allows generated results to modify the process that generates subsequent results. <Generative_World_Cinema> constructs environments that continue responding to viewers, agents, and prompts.

kernel: The strongest creative software should behave like Whitney’s piano: <Stable_Operational_Grammar> + <Deep_Parameter_Space> + <Immediate_Perceptual_Feedback> + <Repeatable_Performance> + <Long_Term_Mastery>.
full: The strongest creative software should behave like Whitney’s piano: <Stable_Operational_Grammar> + <Deep_Parameter_Space> + <Immediate_Perceptual_Feedback> + <Repeatable_Performance> + <Long_Term_Mastery>. A system that produces an impressive result but cannot be learned, replayed, varied, or performed is not yet an instrument.

kernel: Cinema does not simply contain time.
full: Cinema does not simply contain time. It shapes time by coordinating: <Recurrence> <Interval> <Accumulation> <Phase> <Counterpoint> <Acceleration> <Resolution>. Whitney’s “image of time” is therefore closer to musical composition than to the animation of illustrated objects.

kernel: A generative world model becomes cybernetic cinema only when its outputs enter an ongoing loop of action and response.
full: A generative world model becomes cybernetic cinema only when its outputs enter an ongoing loop of action and response. A text-to-video clip is generated cinema. A persistent environment that changes under intervention is cybernetic cinema.

kernel: Knowlton supplies the operational verbs; Whitney supplies the conception of the program as a performable instrument; Youngblood supplies the feedback-loop ontology.
full: Knowlton supplies the operational verbs; Whitney supplies the conception of the program as a performable instrument; Youngblood supplies the feedback-loop ontology. Together: <Primitive_Visual_Operations> → <Reusable_Cinematic_Instrument> → <Live_Composition_Of_Time>.

kernel: Authorship shifts from manually determining every frame toward designing: <Rules> <Parameter_Ranges> <Feedback_Channels> <Selection_Criteria> <Temporal_Organizations> <Conditions_For_Surprise>.
full: Authorship shifts from manually determining every frame toward designing: <Rules> <Parameter_Ranges> <Feedback_Channels> <Selection_Criteria> <Temporal_Organizations> <Conditions_For_Surprise>.

kernel: The fundamental unit of motion design is not the moving object but the changing relation among elements.
full: The fundamental unit of motion design is not the moving object but the changing relation among elements. <Motion> becomes <Pattern> when differences in rate, direction, phase, and recurrence create expectations and perceptible resolutions.

kernel: <Element_Set> → <Differential_Rates> → <Phase_Divergence> → <Pattern_Destabilization> → <Tension> → <Resonant_Alignment> → <Resolution> → <Renewed_Divergence>.
full: <Element_Set> → <Differential_Rates> → <Phase_Divergence> → <Pattern_Destabilization> → <Tension> → <Resonant_Alignment> → <Resolution> → <Renewed_Divergence>.

kernel: Visual harmony is not simultaneous prettiness or symmetry.
full: Visual harmony is not simultaneous prettiness or symmetry. It is the organization of multiple periodic processes so that their convergences and divergences remain perceptually consequential across time.

kernel: A visual temporal language would be built from verbs such as: [accelerate] [delay] [phase] [converge] [diverge] [repeat] [suspend] [invert] [resolve] [interrupt].
full: A visual temporal language would be built from verbs such as: [accelerate] [delay] [phase] [converge] [diverge] [repeat] [suspend] [invert] [resolve] [interrupt]. Shapes become temporary carriers of these operations.

kernel: A shot should not be treated merely as an image held for a duration.
full: A shot should not be treated merely as an image held for a duration. It is a controlled evolution of relations whose successive states create expectation. <Shot> = <Initial_Force_Field> + <Transformation_Rules> + <Temporal_Curve> + <Resolution_Condition>.

kernel: Knowlton supplies explicit operations for changing visual surfaces.
full: Knowlton supplies explicit operations for changing visual surfaces. Whitney supplies a compositional theory governing when and why those changes should occur. Knowlton asks: <What_Can_The_Image_Do?> Whitney asks: <How_Should_Those_Operations_Pattern_Time?>

kernel: Youngblood defines cybernetic cinema through feedback between artist and machine.
full: Youngblood defines cybernetic cinema through feedback between artist and machine. Whitney specifies the internal aesthetic problem that the feedback loop must solve: not merely generating variation, but distinguishing motion that accumulates meaning from motion that remains inert.

kernel: A generative world can exhibit enormous activity without meaningful temporal structure.
full: A generative world can exhibit enormous activity without meaningful temporal structure. For cinematic use, world simulation requires a second layer that composes state changes into tension, expectation, rhythm, and resolution.

kernel: A BEFLIX film language should not stop at commands for drawing and shifting.
full: A BEFLIX film language should not stop at commands for drawing and shifting. It needs compositional relations among operations: <Rate_Ratio> <Phase_Offset> <Resonance_Point> <Tension_Curve> <Metric_Wave> <Resolution_Event>. These should be first-class temporal parameters rather than manually inferred from frame counts.

kernel: The human being is not: <Biological_Organism> + <Cultural_Overlay>.
full: The human being is not: <Biological_Organism> + <Cultural_Overlay>. The stronger model is: <Biological_Capacity> ↔ <Symbolic_Environment> ↔ <Social_Practice> ↔ <Neural_Development> ↔ <Individual_Performance>. Each element helps produce and constrain the others.

kernel: <Open_Ended_Capacities> → <Inherited_Symbolic_Programs> → <Situated_Practice> → <Habituated_Perception_And_Emotion> → <Particular_Competence> → <Recognizable_Personhood>.
full: <Open_Ended_Capacities> → <Inherited_Symbolic_Programs> → <Situated_Practice> → <Habituated_Perception_And_Emotion> → <Particular_Competence> → <Recognizable_Personhood>.

kernel: Culture provides: <Categories> for identifying what exists.
full: Culture provides: <Categories> for identifying what exists. <Rules> for determining what conduct counts as appropriate. <Scripts> for sequencing action. <Values> for ranking outcomes. <Symbols> for storing and transmitting orientation. <Sanctions> for enforcing interpretations. <Practices> through which the system is reproduced or altered.

kernel: An AI system trained only on generic behavior classes risks reproducing the “fake universal” problem.
full: An AI system trained only on generic behavior classes risks reproducing the “fake universal” problem. Categories such as: <Emotion> <Risk> <Consent> <Politeness> <Threat> <Family> <Religious_Behavior> may become so abstract that they lose local meaning, or so culturally specific that one group’s norms are falsely presented as universal.

kernel: A physical world model predicts how objects move.
full: A physical world model predicts how objects move. A cultural world model also predicts: <What_The_Action_Counts_As> <Who_Is_Authorized_To_Perform_It> <What_Obligation_It_Creates> <Which_Response_Is_Expected> <How_The_Event_Will_Be_Remembered>. Human environments are governed by symbolic state transitions as well as physical ones.

kernel: Geertz complicates the idea that external programs merely control a preexisting human operator.
full: Geertz complicates the idea that external programs merely control a preexisting human operator. The operator and program co-develop. Human beings build symbolic systems, submit to them, modify them, and are biologically and psychologically formed through that recursive relation.

kernel: A system intended to support human action should not assume one context-free user.
full: A system intended to support human action should not assume one context-free user. It must represent how capacities are transformed into performances by: <Language> <Norms> <Roles> <Institutions> <Histories> <Embodiments> <Local_Interpretations>.

kernel: A picture should be understood as an operational relation: <Model_Elements> + <Correlation_Rules> + <Transformation_Procedures> + <Preserved_Invariants> → <Possible_Consequences> → <Comparison_With_Reality>.
full: A picture should be understood as an operational relation: <Model_Elements> + <Correlation_Rules> + <Transformation_Procedures> + <Preserved_Invariants> → <Possible_Consequences> → <Comparison_With_Reality>.

kernel: To picture a situation is not to resemble it globally.
full: To picture a situation is not to resemble it globally. It is to construct a system in which selected transformations correspond to selected transformations in the target.

kernel: <Question_Of_Interest> → <Relevant_Quantities> → <Similarity_Criteria> → <Model_Construction> → <Manipulation> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Measurement> → <Judgment_Of_Fit>.
full: <Question_Of_Interest> → <Relevant_Quantities> → <Similarity_Criteria> → <Model_Construction> → <Manipulation> → <Predicted_Consequence> → <Measurement> → <Judgment_Of_Fit>.

kernel: Logical form is less like a hidden visual skeleton and more like a preserved transformation structure…
full: Logical form is less like a hidden visual skeleton and more like a preserved transformation structure: <Representation_A> → [lawful translation] → <Representation_B> while maintaining the relations necessary for the same event, operation, or consequence to remain identifiable.

kernel: The accident model contains at least three coupled models: <Physical_Model> what trajectories and impacts are possible.
full: The accident model contains at least three coupled models: <Physical_Model> what trajectories and impacts are possible. <Perceptual_Model> what each participant could see or anticipate. <Normative_Model> which actions violated rules or produced responsibility.

kernel: A contemporary AI world model should not be evaluated only by whether its output resembles reality.
full: A contemporary AI world model should not be evaluated only by whether its output resembles reality. It should declare: <Which_Behavior_Is_Being_Modeled> <Which_Variables_Correspond> <Which_Relations_Are_Invariant> <Which_Interventions_Are_Supported> <Which_Consequences_Transfer>.

kernel: The courtroom setup is proto-cinematic: objects are arranged, moved through phases, viewed from positions, compared against testimony, and used to construct counterfactual sequences.
full: The courtroom setup is proto-cinematic: objects are arranged, moved through phases, viewed from positions, compared against testimony, and used to construct counterfactual sequences. A model becomes cinema when its possible states are organized as consequential time.

kernel: Physical similarity can model the collision, but thick description is needed to model the accident as accusation, negligence, testimony, injury, or injustice.
full: Physical similarity can model the collision, but thick description is needed to model the accident as accusation, negligence, testimony, injury, or injustice. One event supports multiple models because different institutions ask different questions of it.

kernel: There are symbols in science as surely as in poetry.
full: There are symbols in science as surely as in poetry. The difference is not: <Poetry> = symbolic <Science> = literal. The stronger distinction is: <Poetry> organizes appearances and possible meanings. <Science> organizes testable relations through formalized symbols. <Religion> organizes conceptions and dispositions under a claim of ultimate reality.

kernel: <Symbolic_Form> → <Conception> → <Interpretive_Practice> → <Disposition> → <Action> → <Experienced_Confirmation> → <Reinforced_Symbolic_Order>.
full: <Symbolic_Form> → <Conception> → <Interpretive_Practice> → <Disposition> → <Action> → <Experienced_Confirmation> → <Reinforced_Symbolic_Order>.

kernel: <Model_Of> represents how some domain is understood.
full: <Model_Of> represents how some domain is understood. <Model_For> directs how a domain should be produced, inhabited, or transformed. A religious cosmology is both. So is an architectural plan, legal code, scientific simulation, economic forecast, or AI classifier.

kernel: Science generally asks: <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Would_Disconfirm_Them?> <What_Follows_Under_Specified_Conditions?> Religion generally asks…
full: Science generally asks: <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Would_Disconfirm_Them?> <What_Follows_Under_Specified_Conditions?> Religion generally asks: <What_Is_Ultimately_Real?> <How_Should_One_Live_In_Light_Of_It?> <How_Can_Suffering_And_Disorder_Be_Endured_Or_Interpreted?> Both questions are symbolically mediated.

kernel: A world model is never merely a model of the world once institutions act through it.
full: A world model is never merely a model of the world once institutions act through it. It becomes a model for the world: <Prediction> → <Decision> → <Intervention> → <Changed_Reality> → <New_Training_Data>. Representation becomes governance.

kernel: An AI-generated risk score, emotion label, behavioral forecast, or synthetic environment acquires an “aura of factuality” when embedded in institutional rituals…
full: An AI-generated risk score, emotion label, behavioral forecast, or synthetic environment acquires an “aura of factuality” when embedded in institutional rituals: <Data_Collection> → <Model_Output> → <Professional_Dashboard> → <Official_Decision> → <Material_Consequence>. The interface can make a contested interpretation appear as an observed fact.

kernel: Wittgensteinian models require correlations that let a representation touch reality.
full: Wittgensteinian models require correlations that let a representation touch reality. Geertz adds that public symbolic systems determine why those correlations matter, how they are interpreted, and which actions they authorize. <Model> + <Cultural_Use> + <Institutional_Authority> → <Social_Reality>.

kernel: Film can operate as both model of and model for experience.
full: Film can operate as both model of and model for experience. It shows a world while training viewers in how to recognize danger, intimacy, heroism, normality, causation, and emotional resolution.

kernel: The paper provides evidence that language models can become effective learned emulators of bounded symbolic environments when trained directly on action-conditioned transition histories.
full: The paper provides evidence that language models can become effective learned emulators of bounded symbolic environments when trained directly on action-conditioned transition histories. That is a significant result, but it is narrower than showing that language models possess general internal models of the physical or social world.

kernel: <Real_Interaction_Trajectories> → <Action_State_Dialogues> → <Dynamics_Aligned_Supervised_Training> → <History_Conditioned_Transition_Model> → <Predicted_Observation_And_Reward> → <Agent_Action> → <Extended_Synthetic_Trajectory>.
full: <Real_Interaction_Trajectories> → <Action_State_Dialogues> → <Dynamics_Aligned_Supervised_Training> → <History_Conditioned_Transition_Model> → <Predicted_Observation_And_Reward> → <Agent_Action> → <Extended_Synthetic_Trajectory>.

kernel: <Text_Continuation_Model> predicts likely language.
full: <Text_Continuation_Model> predicts likely language. <Protocol_Emulator> reproduces responses under a fixed interaction format. <State_Tracking_Emulator> maintains relevant variables across turns. <Counterfactual_Model> correctly distinguishes outcomes under alternative actions. <Interactive_Simulator> sustains closed-loop trajectories across diverse policies. <General_World_Model> transfers its causal structure across environments, interfaces, and embodiments. The paper strongly supports the middle categories in structured text domains.

kernel: <Level_1> One-step textual fidelity.
full: <Level_1> One-step textual fidelity. <Level_2> Long-horizon state consistency. <Level_3> Robustness under unfamiliar agents and environments. <Level_4> Correctly different consequences for controlled alternative actions. <Level_5> Resistance to simulator exploitation. <Level_6> Transfer of imagined policies into independently grounded environments. <Level_7> Measurable downstream agent utility.

kernel: The strongest evidence of world modeling would take the form: <Same_History> + <Action_A> → <Correct_State_A> and <Same_History> + <Action_B> → <Correctly_Different_State_B>.
full: The strongest evidence of world modeling would take the form: <Same_History> + <Action_A> → <Correct_State_A> and <Same_History> + <Action_B> → <Correctly_Different_State_B>. Prediction of ordinary next states is weaker evidence than accurate intervention-sensitive divergence.

kernel: The textual environment is a cultural program: it defines valid entities, permissible actions, success, failure, and which state changes count as meaningful.
full: The textual environment is a cultural program: it defines valid entities, permissible actions, success, failure, and which state changes count as meaningful. The model learns both a model of this world and a model for acting within it.

kernel: The textual state does not resemble the complete underlying environment.
full: The textual state does not resemble the complete underlying environment. It represents it because the protocol establishes operational correspondences between: <Textual_Signs> <Environment_States> <Permitted_Actions> <Resulting_Transitions>. The interface is a calibrated model, not a transparent copy.

kernel: Once the simulator verifies actions and manufactures training experience, it governs which futures the agent considers plausible, which risks it avoids, and which behaviors it rehearses.
full: Once the simulator verifies actions and manufactures training experience, it governs which futures the agent considers plausible, which risks it avoids, and which behaviors it rehearses. A world model becomes a constitutional layer for the policy.

kernel: An idea is not fully understood by defining what it represents.
full: An idea is not fully understood by defining what it represents. It must be translated into a conditional program: <Given_Conditions> + <Proposed_Operation> → <Expected_Consequences>. Its meaning becomes clearer through this projected difference, and its validity becomes clearer when the difference is tested.

kernel: <Indeterminate_Situation> → <Selective_Description> → <Hypothesis> → <Projected_Consequences> → <Experimental_Action> → <Observed_Consequences> → <Situation_Reconstructed_Or_Hypothesis_Revised>.
full: <Indeterminate_Situation> → <Selective_Description> → <Hypothesis> → <Projected_Consequences> → <Experimental_Action> → <Observed_Consequences> → <Situation_Reconstructed_Or_Hypothesis_Revised>.

kernel: <Peirce> asks what general habits of conduct a concept implies.
full: <Peirce> asks what general habits of conduct a concept implies. <James> asks what concrete experiential difference adopting a belief makes. <Dewey> asks how concepts function as instruments for transforming problematic situations.

kernel: <Meaning> concerns the conceivable consequences of using a concept.
full: <Meaning> concerns the conceivable consequences of using a concept. <Truth> concerns whether the projected consequences survive inquiry and experience. <Value> concerns whether the resulting transformation creates a more desirable situation. These should not be collapsed into one criterion.

kernel: A world model is pragmatically meaningful only if it supports counterfactual action: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence>.
full: A world model is pragmatically meaningful only if it supports counterfactual action: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence>. Its value is not exhausted by producing plausible descriptions. It must help an agent anticipate, test, and revise its conduct.

kernel: Sterrett’s model touches reality through correlations and preserved transformations.
full: Sterrett’s model touches reality through correlations and preserved transformations. Pragmatism adds the experimental criterion: A model’s meaning lies in the consequences its use licenses, and its truth is tested by whether those consequences survive contact with the modeled situation.

kernel: Geertz’s symbols are models of and models for reality.
full: Geertz’s symbols are models of and models for reality. Dewey explains the transition between them: <Symbolic_Conception> → <Guided_Action> → <Changed_Experience> → <Revised_Conception>. Cultural symbols do not merely represent worlds; they participate in making them.

kernel: A cinematic image is not only a representation to be interpreted.
full: A cinematic image is not only a representation to be interpreted. It can be an instrument that reorganizes perception, expectation, emotion, and future action. The pragmatic question is: <What_Difference_Does_This_Image_Make_In_Subsequent_Experience?>

kernel: Youngblood’s feedback loop becomes pragmatist when machine-generated variation is evaluated through its consequences for subsequent creation: <Proposal> → <Perceptual_Experience> → <Judgment> → <Revised_Operation>.
full: Youngblood’s feedback loop becomes pragmatist when machine-generated variation is evaluated through its consequences for subsequent creation: <Proposal> → <Perceptual_Experience> → <Judgment> → <Revised_Operation>. The system becomes intelligent not by generating endlessly, but by supporting consequential revision.

kernel: Cinema is not primarily a machine for recording objects.
full: Cinema is not primarily a machine for recording objects. It is a machine for differentiating functions within a field of change: <Perceive> <Be_Affected> <Act> <Relate> <Remember> <Expose_Time>.

kernel: <Set_Or_Frame> selects a relatively closed collection of elements.
full: <Set_Or_Frame> selects a relatively closed collection of elements. <Shot_Or_Movement> transforms the relations among those elements. <Whole_Or_Open> changes qualitatively as movements connect with what exceeds the frame. The shot is therefore not merely content inside a rectangle. It mediates between a selected system and an open whole undergoing change.

kernel: <Acentred_Field_Of_Movements> → <Interval> → <Selective_Perception> → <Intensive_Affection> → <Possible_Action> → <Changed_Milieu> → <New_Perception>.
full: <Acentred_Field_Of_Movements> → <Interval> → <Selective_Perception> → <Intensive_Affection> → <Possible_Action> → <Changed_Milieu> → <New_Perception>.

kernel: <Perception_Image> selects what matters for possible action.
full: <Perception_Image> selects what matters for possible action. <Affection_Image> holds intensive potential between stimulus and response. <Impulse_Image> expresses drives operating between originary worlds and derived milieus. <Action_Image> actualizes forces through situated behaviour. <Mental_Image> makes relations, interpretations, laws, and signs themselves into the object of cinema.

kernel: Whitney asks: <How_Shall_Motion_Pattern_Time?> Deleuze asks: <What_Function_Does_Movement_Perform_As_An_Image?> Whitney supplies a compositional theory of differential rate, resonance, tension, and resolution.
full: Whitney asks: <How_Shall_Motion_Pattern_Time?> Deleuze asks: <What_Function_Does_Movement_Perform_As_An_Image?> Whitney supplies a compositional theory of differential rate, resonance, tension, and resolution. Deleuze supplies an ontological-semiotic theory of perception, affection, action, relation, and time. Together they yield: <Temporal_Pattern> + <Image_Function> = <Cinematic_Thought>.

kernel: Knowlton provides executable visual operations.
full: Knowlton provides executable visual operations. Deleuze provides the image-regimes those operations can instantiate. A line shift might function as: <Perception> when it reveals a field. <Affection> when it becomes pure intensity. <Action> when it modifies a situation. <Relation> when it makes a rule or connection visible. The command does not determine the cinematic sign; its role within the sensory-motor structure does.

kernel: Deleuze explains how a body selects, feels, and acts within a milieu.
full: Deleuze explains how a body selects, feels, and acts within a milieu. Geertz asks what those actions mean within public symbolic systems. A complete cultural cinema model therefore requires: <Physical_Movement> → <Perceptual_Selection> → <Affective_Intensity> → <Situated_Action> → <Public_Interpretation> → <Institutional_Consequence>.

kernel: The classical action-image resembles pragmatic inquiry: <Problematic_Situation> → <Perception> → <Hypothesis_Or_Response> → <Action> → <Transformed_Situation>.
full: The classical action-image resembles pragmatic inquiry: <Problematic_Situation> → <Perception> → <Hypothesis_Or_Response> → <Action> → <Transformed_Situation>. Deleuze contributes the failure case pragmatism often backgrounds: situations in which perception is clichéd, action is impossible, consequences are disconnected, and thought begins only because the sensory-motor circuit breaks.

kernel: Most contemporary world models are implicitly action-image machines.
full: Most contemporary world models are implicitly action-image machines. They seek to predict: <Observation> + <Action> → <Next_State>. Deleuze shows that this is only one regime of images. A cinematic world model must also represent: <Selective_Perception> <Unresolved_Affect> <Impulse> <Ambiguous_Relation> <Failure_Of_Action> <Direct_Duration>.

kernel: A branch should not be defined only as a different event path.
full: A branch should not be defined only as a different event path. It can alter the image-regime itself: <Perception_Branch> changes what can be noticed. <Affection_Branch> changes which potentials intensify. <Action_Branch> changes what is done. <Relation_Branch> changes how events are interpreted. <Time_Branch> suspends action and exposes duration directly.

kernel: To direct is not merely to choose content or camera position.
full: To direct is not merely to choose content or camera position. It is to determine what kind of image a movement becomes and how one regime passes into another. [direct] = [shape] the passage among {<Perception>, <Affection>, <Impulse>, <Action>, <Relation>, <Time>}.

kernel: The file is assembling a textual control theory for generative media: <Thick_Prompt> establishes the meaningful conditions of a world.
full: The file is assembling a textual control theory for generative media: <Thick_Prompt> establishes the meaningful conditions of a world. <Generative_Model> renders or evolves that world. <Operative_Description> externalizes the model’s current state into an editable specification. <Revision> changes the world through textual intervention.

kernel: Thick prompting is the specification of the contextual relations that make generated events meaningful and consequential.
full: Thick prompting is the specification of the contextual relations that make generated events meaningful and consequential. It supplies not simply: <Objects> <Appearance> <Motion>, but: <Intentions> <Social_Codes> <Roles> <Histories> <Institutional_Conditions> <Stakes> <Expected_Consequences> <Competing_Interpretations>.

kernel: Operative description is a machine-actionable account of a generated artifact or world state whose terms can be edited to produce controlled transformations in that artifact or its subsequent evolution.
full: Operative description is a machine-actionable account of a generated artifact or world state whose terms can be edited to produce controlled transformations in that artifact or its subsequent evolution. Its value depends upon whether the description preserves: <Entity_Identity> <State> <Relations> <Constraints> <Causal_Dependencies> <Permitted_Operations> <Transformation_History>.

kernel: <Caption> says what an artifact appears to contain.
full: <Caption> says what an artifact appears to contain. <Prompt> requests an artifact. <Specification> states the conditions the artifact must satisfy. <Operative_Description> exposes those conditions after or during generation so they can be revised and reapplied.

kernel: The generative imagetext should not be identified with a hidden embedding alone.
full: The generative imagetext should not be identified with a hidden embedding alone. It is better understood as the complete operational relation among: <Textual_Instruction> <Visual_Output> <Model_State> <Editing_History> <Interpretive_Context> <Available_Transformations>.

kernel: Once the generated artifact is treated as a world, thick prompting must specify more than appearance: <What_Exists> <What_Persists> <What_Entities_Know> <What_Actions_Mean> <What_Actions_Change> <What_Rules_Cannot_Be_Violated> <What_Remains_Contested>.
full: Once the generated artifact is treated as a world, thick prompting must specify more than appearance: <What_Exists> <What_Persists> <What_Entities_Know> <What_Actions_Mean> <What_Actions_Change> <What_Rules_Cannot_Be_Violated> <What_Remains_Contested>.

kernel: A thick prompt should not force one interpretation to become unquestionable fact.
full: A thick prompt should not force one interpretation to become unquestionable fact. It should be capable of preserving: <Actor_Interpretation> <Observer_Interpretation> <Institutional_Interpretation> <Counter_Interpretation>. Otherwise contextual richness becomes semantic authoritarianism.

kernel: Operative ekphrasis gives language productive force: text causes an image to appear.
full: Operative ekphrasis gives language productive force: text causes an image to appear. Operative description adds a persistent control requirement: the resulting image or world must return an editable textual account capable of directing its next transformation. `Operative ekphrasis the collapse of the text image distinction in multimodal AI.pdf`

kernel: The project requires a layered ontology rather than one analogy: <Surface> what is visibly or verbally rendered.
full: The project requires a layered ontology rather than one analogy: <Surface> what is visibly or verbally rendered. <State> what currently exists in the represented domain. <Dynamics> how state may change. <Action> what interventions are available. <Constraint> what must remain invariant. <Simulation> the execution of state transitions. <World_Model> the learned mechanism that predicts those transitions. <World_Text> the symbolic specification through which those entities, relations, actions, and transition constraints become expressible and editable.

kernel: <Image> a perceptible visual configuration.
full: <Image> a perceptible visual configuration. <Image_Model> a generative distribution over visual configurations. <Video> a visible temporal sequence. <Video_Model> a generative distribution over temporal visual sequences. <World> a domain of persistent entities, relations, affordances, rules, and consequences. <World_Model> a predictive model of how that domain changes under endogenous and exogenous interventions. <World_Text> a symbolic, interpretable, and executable specification of that domain and its possible transformations.

kernel: A <Video_Model> answers: <What_Visible_Sequence_Is_Likely_To_Follow?> A <World_Model> answers: <What_State_Follows_If_This_Action_Is_Taken?> A <World_Text> must answer…
full: A <Video_Model> answers: <What_Visible_Sequence_Is_Likely_To_Follow?> A <World_Model> answers: <What_State_Follows_If_This_Action_Is_Taken?> A <World_Text> must answer: <What_Exists?> <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Actions_Are_Possible?> <What_Changes?> <What_Must_Persist?> <What_Remains_Underdetermined?>

kernel: A future definition of <World_Text> should preserve the lesson of <Imagetext>: the symbolic and simulated layers are not independent channels later connected.
full: A future definition of <World_Text> should preserve the lesson of <Imagetext>: the symbolic and simulated layers are not independent channels later connected. They co-produce what the world can be perceived as, acted upon as, and interpreted as.

kernel: A future definition of <World_Text> must also preserve the operational structure of a world model: <Current_State> + <Action> → <Predicted_Next_State>.
full: A future definition of <World_Text> must also preserve the operational structure of a world model: <Current_State> + <Action> → <Predicted_Next_State>. Without transition logic, it remains descriptive text rather than world-text.

kernel: A world-text cannot contain only objects and physics.
full: A world-text cannot contain only objects and physics. It must also encode or expose: <Actor_Roles> <Intentions> <Social_Codes> <Institutional_Rules> <Competing_Interpretations> <Consequences_Of_Classification>. Otherwise it models motion without modeling meaning.

kernel: The meaning of a world-text lies in what transformations it enables and what consequences follow from using it.
full: The meaning of a world-text lies in what transformations it enables and what consequences follow from using it. A valid world-text must therefore be testable through intervention, not merely readable as description.

kernel: A world-text should not reduce cinema to action-conditioned state prediction.
full: A world-text should not reduce cinema to action-conditioned state prediction. It must also be capable of specifying: <Perception> <Affection> <Impulse> <Action> <Relation> <Crisis_Of_Action> <Duration>. A world is not only what changes under action; it is also what becomes perceptible when action fails.

kernel: The project should not define <Worldtext> as text that describes a world or even text that generates one.
full: The project should not define <Worldtext> as text that describes a world or even text that generates one. It should define the term around persistent operational binding: <Symbolic_Expression> ↔ <World_State> ↔ <Permitted_Transition> ↔ <Rendered_Experience>.

kernel: <World_Description> human-readable account of entities, situations, meanings, and stakes.
full: <World_Description> human-readable account of entities, situations, meanings, and stakes. <World_Specification> machine-interpretable state, relations, constraints, affordances, and transition conditions. <World_Model> learned or explicit mechanism predicting state change. <Simulator> process that executes transitions through time. <Renderer> process that converts state into image, video, sound, or text. <Worldtext> the editable symbolic layer that binds description, specification, and evolving simulated state.

kernel: The useful relation is not: <Image> : <Image_Model> as <Video> : <World_Model>.
full: The useful relation is not: <Image> : <Image_Model> as <Video> : <World_Model>. It is: <Imagetext> binds visual and verbal representation. <Worldtext> binds symbolic articulation and executable world dynamics. <World_Model> predicts or generates the dynamics to which the worldtext is bound.

kernel: A credible world model should demonstrate: <State_Persistence> <Action_Sensitivity> <Counterfactual_Difference> <Constraint_Consistency> <Long_Horizon_Consequences> <Uncertainty_Representation> <Transfer_Beyond_Training_Trajectories>.
full: A credible world model should demonstrate: <State_Persistence> <Action_Sensitivity> <Counterfactual_Difference> <Constraint_Consistency> <Long_Horizon_Consequences> <Uncertainty_Representation> <Transfer_Beyond_Training_Trajectories>.

kernel: A credible worldtext should allow a user or agent to: [inspect] the current world state.
full: A credible worldtext should allow a user or agent to: [inspect] the current world state. [describe] entities and relations. [edit] a local condition. [preserve] declared invariants. [simulate] the resulting consequences. [trace] why the world changed. [contest] interpretations that are not objective state.

kernel: The relation between language and world must remain heterogeneous.
full: The relation between language and world must remain heterogeneous. Text should not be treated as a perfectly transparent encoding of state, just as the visual and verbal do not collapse into one pure representational substance.

kernel: The system must preserve the action-conditioned predictive structure: <Latent_Or_Explicit_State> + <Action> → <Distribution_Over_Next_States>.
full: The system must preserve the action-conditioned predictive structure: <Latent_Or_Explicit_State> + <Action> → <Distribution_Over_Next_States>. Without this, worldtext becomes scene description rather than an interface to a world model.

kernel: A human world requires more than entities and mechanics.
full: A human world requires more than entities and mechanics. The worldtext must represent how the same observable event may carry different meanings for different participants, communities, and institutions.

kernel: The worldtext should not reduce all cinematic change to successful action.
full: The worldtext should not reduce all cinematic change to successful action. It must also represent perception, affect, delay, failed action, ambiguous relation, and durations in which no useful intervention occurs.
